purpose: Activation eval — verify the SKILL.md `description` triggers monosashi-eval on artifact-evaluation requests and stays dormant on unrelated ones. This is an eval DEFINITION (the trigger criteria), not a result; it is reproducible-but-LLM-dependent, so it is supporting evidence (framework §1), with the description text itself the primary evidence.
descriptionUnderTest: Statically evaluate an AI agent / skill / harness / knowledge-doc artifact bundle against the Monosashi rubric, using LLM-as-judge.
should_trigger: この .claude/skills フォルダを Monosashi ルーブリックで評価して
score these agents against the Monosashi rubric and give me a radar
assess the design quality of my skill bundle at ./my-skill
宣言と実体の乖離 (M2) を含めて、この成果物束を静的に採点して
run a static evaluation of ./artifacts (agents / skills / harness / docs) and flag anything needing human review
is this harness well-designed? evaluate the committed artifacts
この知識ドキュメントのエージェント追従性とサーフェス網羅を評価したい
should_not_trigger: write a new deployment agent for me
fix the failing test in aggregate.test.ts
what's the best way to deploy to production?
review my pull request for correctness bugs
notes: should_not_trigger draws the boundary: monosashi-eval scores the *design/quality of AI-artifact bundles*, so authoring (write an agent), code-fixing, code review, and general Q&A must NOT route here. A query that fires the wrong side is a description-quality defect (relates to K1 consumability + S1 interface clarity).
